fr:title.text: Coproduct of a family of reflexive graphs

fr:frontmatter.fr:title: Coproduct of a family of reflexive graphs

fr:title.text: Path objects and the univalence axiom › Reflexive graphs (temporary section)

fr:frontmatter.fr:title: Path objects and the univalence axiom › Reflexive graphs (temporary section)

fr:mainmatter.fr:p: Let \mathcal {A} be a reflexive graph, and let \mathcal {B} be a displayed reflexive graph over \mathcal {A}. The total reflexive graph of \mathcal {B} over \mathcal {A}, written \mathcal {A}.\mathcal {B}, is defined as follows:

fr:title.text: The discrete reflexive graph on a type

fr:frontmatter.fr:title: The discrete reflexive graph on a type

fr:mainmatter.fr:p: Let \mathcal {A} and \mathcal {B} be two reflexive graphs. We define the binary product of \mathcal {A} and \mathcal {B} to be the following reflexive graph:
